title: A Doll's House : a play
author: Ibsen, Henrik
summary: Nora Helmer returns home cheerful, arranging Christmas gifts while her husband Torvald teases her with pet names and cautions against debt. They discuss finances, his upcoming bank appointment, and the household’s prospects. Nora playfully pleads for money to save as a personal gift and hides a special parcel. A porter and maid attend, and Mrs. Linde unexpectedly arrives, older and widowed. Nora recounts their salvage during earlier hardship, mentioning needlework and a year in Italy to restore Torvald’s health. The scene establishes domestic intimacy, gendered patronage, social respectability, and hints of unstated sacrifices behind Nora’s lighthearted manner and future tensions.
